admin.confirmDeleteTenant: Delete tenant "{name}" and all of its data? This cannot be undone.
admin.searchTenants: Search by name, slug or creator…

empty.tenantsHint: Click "New tenant" to create the first client company.

modal.createTenantSubtitle: Fill in the company and owner info. We generate the password and a ready-to-send invite message.
modal.tenantReadyDesc: {company} is ready. Send the credentials below to the owner.
modal.createAdminSubtitle: Promote an existing user or create a new one. If new, we generate the password and welcome message.
modal.adminCreatedTitle: Super admin created. Send the credentials below.
modal.credsHint: Ask the user to change their password on first sign-in.

errors.invalid_name: Enter a name with at least 2 characters.
errors.weak_password: Password must be at least 8 characters.
errors.cannot_demote_self: You can't remove your own privilege.
errors.cannot_demote_hiper: You can't modify the platform creator's account.
errors.forbidden: You don't have permission for this action.

auth.loginSubtitle: Sign in to your account and pick up where you left off.
auth.registerSubtitle: Your AI will be working your leads in a few minutes.
auth.trialInfo: Includes a 7-day free trial. Cancel anytime.
auth.loginFailed: We couldn't sign you in. Try again.
auth.rateLimited: Too many attempts. Wait a moment and try again.
auth.registrationFailed: We couldn't create the account. Try again.
auth.validationFailed: Check the fields and try again.
auth.checkoutCanceled: Payment cancelled. You can try again whenever you want.
auth.oauthFailed: Failed to sign in with the selected provider.
auth.passwordMinLength: Password must be at least 8 characters.

dashboard.title: Dashboard
dashboard.subtitle: Overview of your AI-powered digital marketing

empty.heroSubtitle: Set up your AI in three quick steps and start converting leads today.
empty.step1Desc: Define the AI's goal, the channel and the tone.
empty.step2Desc: WhatsApp, email or SMS, pick where the AI will talk.
empty.step3Desc: Upload the ad so the AI understands what was promised to the lead.

goalHero.emptyTitle: Set up your funnel to get started
goalHero.emptyDesc: Pick the AI's goal in the sales funnel and track here the percentage of leads that reach it.

conversations.title: Conversations
conversations.subtitle: Monitor your AI conversations with leads
conversations.noConversationsDescription: Conversations will appear when leads arrive
conversations.selectConversationDesc: Choose a conversation to monitor the AI
conversations.failNoWhatsapp: this number does not have WhatsApp
conversations.failNoWhatsappHint: The lead's number is not registered on WhatsApp. Resending will not help. Reach this lead via email or SMS if those channels are enabled.
conversations.failInstanceOfflineHint: The WhatsApp connection dropped. Go to Channels -> WhatsApp and click Restart. If that does not fix it, click Reconnect and scan the QR again.
conversations.failChannelMisconfiguredHint: The channel is missing data (instance or Evolution URL). Go to Channels -> WhatsApp and redo the connection.
conversations.failAuthHint: Evolution refused the API key. Check the key on the server (EVOLUTION_API_KEY env var).

campaigns.title: Campaigns
campaigns.subtitle: Register your marketing campaigns so the AI can contextualize each lead
campaigns.noCampaignsDescription: Register your first campaign so the AI understands your leads' context.
campaigns.newCampaignDesc: Register a campaign for the AI to contextualize support
campaigns.targetCountriesDesc: Which countries is this campaign running in? This defines the AI's language.
campaigns.aiLanguageLabel: Which language will the AI speak in this campaign?
campaigns.aiLanguageHint: By default, the AI speaks the language of the selected country. Use this only if you want to force another language (e.g. running ads in the US to capture Portuguese-speaking leads).
campaigns.campaignContentDesc: Upload the campaign material. The AI will analyze and understand what is being promised to the lead.
campaigns.captionPlaceholder: Paste the ad caption or copy if available...
campaigns.textPlaceholder: Paste the ad copy, script, text here...
campaigns.fileTooLargeUpload: The file is {size} MB. Upload limit is 1 GB. Compress the file or send a shorter clip.
campaigns.audioTooLong: Extracted audio is {size} MB, above Whisper's 25 MB cap. This usually only happens for media longer than 100 minutes, split it into segments and analyze each separately.
campaigns.proxyTooLarge: The server rejected the upload (too large). Try a smaller file or compress it before sending.
campaigns.proxyTimeout: The server took too long to respond. Try again in a moment or send a smaller file.
campaigns.upstreamUnavailable: The analysis service is unavailable right now. Try again in a few minutes.
campaigns.fileTooLargeImage: The image is {size} MB. Reduce it to 20 MB or less.
campaigns.audioCodecError: Could not read the audio from this file. Make sure the format is supported (MP4, MOV, MP3, WAV, M4A, WEBM, OGG).
campaigns.noContentToAnalyze: Add a file or some text before analyzing.

leads.title: Leads
leads.subtitle: All leads captured from your campaigns
leads.noLeadsDesc: Leads will appear when they arrive from your campaigns
leads.retryOneConfirm: Resend the AI's first message to {name}? Failed attempts will be deleted and the AI will try again.
leads.retryAllConfirm: Resend the first message to all {count} failed lead(s)?
leads.retryOneOk: Resend scheduled, the message will reach the lead's WhatsApp in a few seconds.
leads.retryAlreadySent: This lead already received the first message successfully, nothing to do.
leads.retryError: Could not resend, try again in a moment.
leads.firstContactFailedTip: The AI's first message failed to send
leads.firstContactFailedDesc: The AI's first message did not reach this lead. Click below to delete the previous attempt and try again.
leads.noWhatsappTip: This number does not have WhatsApp
leads.noWhatsappDesc: This number is not registered on WhatsApp, so the message could not be delivered. Resending will not help. Consider reaching this lead via email or SMS if those channels are enabled.
leads.instanceOfflineDesc: The WhatsApp connection dropped even after an auto-restart. Go to Channels -> WhatsApp, click Restart and, if that does not work, Reconnect to scan the QR again. Then come back and resend.

superAdminOnboarding.s1Title: Welcome, you are a Super Admin
superAdminOnboarding.s1Desc: You just got access to the platform's admin panel. As a Super Admin, you manage accounts (tenants) for your clients. Each account you create is a fully isolated workspace with its own AI, leads, channels and team.
s1Bullets: No coding required. Every operation happens in the /admin panel.
Each client account you create gets isolated access to their own platform.
You only see and manage the accounts you created yourself.
superAdminOnboarding.s1TitleHiper: Welcome, you are a Hiper Admin
superAdminOnboarding.s1DescHiper: You are the platform owner. You have full access: you can promote/demote Super Admins, see ALL accounts created by ALL Super Admins, and configure everything. Let us walk you through it in 5 screens.
s1BulletsHiper: Only you can promote users to Super Admin (exclusive tab).
You see every account + the creator who built it (creator metadata).
Super Admins only see the accounts they themselves created.
superAdminOnboarding.s2Desc: Your responsibilities as a platform admin:
s2Bullets: Create accounts (tenants) for client companies in seconds.
Define name, slug, plan (Free, Pro, Enterprise) and max user count.
Generate credentials (email + temporary password) to send to the client.
Track which accounts have completed onboarding and how many users each has.
You do NOT see clients' conversations, leads or internal data — full privacy.
superAdminOnboarding.s3Title: How to create the first client account
superAdminOnboarding.s3Desc: Less than 1 minute, in 4 simple steps:
s3Steps: Open the Admin tab in the sidebar, then click Tenants.
Click New Account in the top right.
Fill in the company name, owner email, plan and max users.
Click Create. Copy the credentials and send them to the client — they can log in right away.
superAdminOnboarding.s4Desc: Each client account is an independent workspace with everything isolated:
s4Bullets: Their own AI with separate training and knowledge base.
Their own leads, conversations and channels (WhatsApp, Email, SMS).
Their own team — the client can add their own collaborators.
The client never sees you, other clients, or the /admin area.
You keep control: reactivate, deactivate or adjust the plan at any time.
superAdminOnboarding.s5Desc: You have everything you need to register your first accounts. If you need help, the Help Center is always in the sidebar.

welcome.title: Welcome, {name}!
welcome.subtitle: In just a few minutes your AI will be ready to work your leads. Let's set up the essentials together.

origin.title: Lead origin
origin.desc: Tell us how your leads reach you.

persona.title: AI persona
persona.desc: Pick a profile that speaks like your business.

template.title: Where do your leads come from?
template.subtitle: Choose the scenario that best describes how you capture them. You can change it later in the Funnel.

goal.title: What's the AI's goal?
goal.subtitle: What should the AI do once the lead is qualified?

channel.title: Which channel will the AI use?
channel.subtitle: Pick the primary channel the AI uses to talk to leads.

persona.title: Bring your AI to life
persona.subtitle: Pick a ready-made persona and tweak the name if you want. You can edit everything later in AI Assistant.
persona.businessNamePlaceholder: How you want the AI to introduce itself

sofia.pitch: Warm and professional, perfect for closing deals naturally.

marcos.pitch: Consultative tone, gets the lead's pain and offers technical solutions.

julia.pitch: Welcoming, perfect for sensitive niches like health and wellness.

gabriel.pitch: Energetic and direct, fully focused on booking the meeting.

ana.pitch: Short and objective replies, ideal for high volume.

done.title: All set
done.subtitle: Your AI is ready to work. Let's register your campaigns so it understands what you're selling.

campaigns.title: Register your campaigns
campaigns.desc: Upload your ad creatives. The AI studies them to understand what was promised to the lead.

channels.title: Connect your channels
channels.desc: Plug in WhatsApp, Email or SMS so the AI can start talking.

pipeline.title: Sales Funnel
pipeline.subtitle: Configure the path the AI will follow with each lead
pipeline.selectTemplateFirst: Select where your lead comes from

wizard.switchToFlat: View all steps on a single page

stepLinks.title: Social media and links the AI can share
stepLinks.desc: Register Instagram, Facebook, TikTok, site, portfolio links or any important URL. The AI sends them automatically when the lead asks or when the situation matches the 'when to send' instruction.
stepLinks.empty: No links yet. Click add so the AI can share social media, site or portfolio when the lead asks.
stepLinks.whenPlaceholder: Ex: When the lead asks for visual references, or asks where to find more of our work.

step1.title: Where does your lead come from?
step1.desc: Select the scenario that best fits your business

step2.title: What's the AI's goal?
step2.desc: What should the AI do when it qualifies the lead?

step3.title: When does the AI make first contact?
step3.desc: Wait time after the lead arrives before the AI sends the first message

formProactive.title: Form / Landing Page
formProactive.desc: Lead fills a form and waits. The AI sends the first message automatically.

whatsappReactive.title: Direct WhatsApp
whatsappReactive.desc: Lead clicks the ad button and goes straight to WhatsApp. The AI responds when the lead messages.

quizProactive.title: External Quiz
quizProactive.desc: Lead fills a Typeform, Google Forms or quiz. Data arrives via webhook and the AI initiates contact.

socialReactive.title: Social Media / DM
socialReactive.desc: Lead sends a message via Instagram, Facebook or other social media. The AI responds automatically.

emailNurture.title: Landing Page + Follow-up
emailNurture.desc: Lead signs up on the landing page. The AI follows up via email and/or WhatsApp to nurture and convert.

manualOutbound.title: Manual List / Referral
manualOutbound.desc: Leads imported manually or by referral. The AI makes first contact via WhatsApp or SMS.

closeSale.title: Close the Sale
closeSale.desc: The AI handles the entire negotiation and closes the sale directly, sending payment link or instructions.

scheduleMeeting.title: Schedule a Meeting
scheduleMeeting.desc: The AI qualifies the lead and schedules a meeting with your sales team (human closer).

qualifyTransfer.title: Qualify and Transfer
qualifyTransfer.desc: The AI asks initial questions, qualifies the lead and transfers to a human agent to continue.

collectSend.title: Collect Data and Send Proposal
collectSend.desc: The AI collects lead information and sends a personalized proposal or informational material.

advanced.title: Advanced Settings
advanced.followUpDesc: The AI automatically follows up if the lead doesn't respond
advanced.humanApprovalDesc: Requires human approval before the AI closes the sale or schedules

step4.title: Which channel will the AI use?
step4.desc: Select how the AI will communicate with leads
step4.channelsLabel: Which channels will the AI use
step4.willSend: AI will send through this channel
step4.hint: The AI sends the first message and follow-ups through ALL selected channels. Pick as many as you want.

stepClosing.title: How to close the sale
stepClosing.desc: Define what the AI does when the lead is ready to buy. Pick whether it sends a direct link, qualifies first, hands off to your team, or adapts to context.
stepClosing.modeAutoDesc: The AI chooses between sending the link or escalating to a human based on the conversation. You configure both, it picks the best.
stepClosing.modeDirectLinkDesc: When the lead is ready, the AI sends the closing link (Stripe, Calendly, etc). Fast and impersonal.
stepClosing.modeQualifyFirstDesc: The AI must get answers to your questions before sending the link. Ensures higher-quality conversions.
stepClosing.modeTeamHandoffDesc: The AI captures lead info and hands off to a human seller to generate the next action. For complex products or high-ticket items.
stepClosing.qualifyingQuestionsHint: The AI must get a clear answer to each one before trying to close. Use natural language — it asks them spread throughout the conversation, not as a form.
stepClosing.qualifyingQuestionsPlaceholder: Ex: How many clients do you serve per month?
stepClosing.requiredInfoHint: Data the AI must collect before pinging your team. Goes into the handoff email/webhook payload.
stepClosing.requiredInfoPlaceholder: Ex: Full name, Tax ID, Address
stepClosing.linkHint: URL the AI sends when it decides to close. Stripe Checkout, Calendly, internal form — paste the full URL.
stepClosing.closingMessagePlaceholder: Ex: Here is the link to complete your purchase. Hit me up here afterwards if you need anything.
stepClosing.handoffEmailHint: Who gets the email when the AI asks a human to take over. Can be you, the responsible seller, or a group address.
stepClosing.handoffWebhookHint: Slack, Discord, Make, n8n or Zapier URL to ping when the AI requests a handoff. Receives a full JSON payload.
stepClosing.handoffWaitMessageLabel: Message the AI sends to the lead while waiting
stepClosing.handoffWaitMessagePlaceholder: Ex: Our team is generating your personalized link, hang on, I will get back to you in a minute.
stepClosing.paymentSubtitle: When on, the AI sends the payment instructions, waits for proof (image or text), pings the human(s) in charge via WhatsApp and only releases the lead after a human OK.
stepClosing.paymentInstructionsHint: Exact text the AI sends to the lead at closing time (Pix key, bank details, Zelle, etc). The AI copies 100% of what you write here, never invents.
stepClosing.paymentInstructionsPlaceholder:
Ex:
Zelle: payments@yourbrand.com
Beneficiary: Your Brand LLC
Amount: USD 297.00

After paying, send the receipt screenshot here.
stepClosing.paymentConfirmersLabel: WhatsApp of the person(s) confirming receipt
stepClosing.paymentConfirmersHint: One or more numbers in international format. When the lead sends proof, they get a message from our AI asking for OK. Just reply 'ok' and the lead is released automatically.
stepClosing.paymentWaitMessageLabel: Message while validating with the team
stepClosing.paymentWaitMessagePlaceholder: Ex: Got your receipt. Validating with the team now, I will get back to you in a few minutes.
stepClosing.paymentConfirmedHint: Text the AI sends automatically to the lead once the confirmer replies with OK.
stepClosing.paymentConfirmedPlaceholder: Ex: Payment confirmed, thank you so much for your purchase and trust! Here is the next step...

step5transfer.title: Transfer to human
step5transfer.desc: Configure who the AI should transfer qualified leads to
step5transfer.messagePlaceholder: I'll connect you with our specialist now...

step5calendar.title: Meeting scheduling
step5calendar.desc: The AI will check availability and schedule meetings automatically
step5calendar.hint: The AI will check this calendar to find available slots and create events.

webhook.title: Configure lead intake
webhook.desc: Configure your lead source to send data to this URL
webhook.saveFirst: Save the funnel first to generate the URL
webhook.typeform: In Typeform: Go to Connect → Webhooks → paste the URL above. Name, email and phone fields are detected automatically.
webhook.googleForms: In Google Forms: Use Google Apps Script or Zapier/Make to send data via POST to the URL above when a response is submitted.
webhook.formLP: In your landing page form: Configure the form action to send a JSON POST with name, email and phone fields to the URL above.
webhook.lpFollowup: Configure your landing page to send signup data via POST to the URL above. The AI will follow up via email and/or WhatsApp.
webhook.metaDesc: Native integration coming soon. In the meantime, set up your Meta form to send leads via Zapier, Make or another webhook pointing to the URL above.

firstMessage.title: First message
firstMessage.desc: How the AI should approach the lead on the first interaction
firstMessage.modeInstructionDesc: AI varies the message per lead, no spam feel
firstMessage.modeExactDesc: AI sends exactly this text, no variation
firstMessage.instructionLabel: Tell the AI what to do
firstMessage.instructionPlaceholder: Example: Greet the lead by name, thank them for interest in Course X, ask what their main goal is. Tone: consultative and direct. Max 2 sentences.
firstMessage.instructionHint: Describe just the INTENT. The AI writes a different message for each lead using the name, campaign context and your training.
firstMessage.exactPlaceholder: Use {name} to insert the lead's name
firstMessage.exactHint: Use {name}, {company}, {campaign} as placeholders. Same message for everyone.

followUps.title: Follow-up cadence
followUps.desc: How the AI will reach out again if the lead doesn't reply
followUps.empty: No follow-ups configured. The AI only sends the first message.
followUps.instructionPlaceholder: Example: Ask if the lead is still interested, offer to clear quick questions. Lighter tone, no pressure.

language.title: AI language
language.desc: Lock the language the AI will use to reply to EVERY lead in this funnel. If you leave Auto, the AI guesses from the lead's text.
language.lockedHint: Locked to {lang}. The AI will reply in this language even if the lead writes in another one.

whatsapp.title: WhatsApp
whatsapp.subtitle: Connect your WhatsApp so the AI can serve your leads
whatsapp.aiReady: The AI is ready to serve your leads via WhatsApp
whatsapp.disconnectConfirm: Are you sure you want to disconnect WhatsApp?
whatsapp.scanInstructions: Open WhatsApp on your phone → Menu (⋮) → Linked devices → Link a device
whatsapp.notConnectedDesc: Connect your WhatsApp so the AI can send and receive messages from your leads automatically.
whatsapp.preparingDesc: Creating instance and generating QR Code
whatsapp.secureConnectionDesc: Your connection is encrypted and secure. No messages are stored outside the system.
whatsapp.autoReplyDesc: Once connected, the AI starts serving your leads automatically via WhatsApp.
whatsapp.useNormallyDesc: You can continue using your WhatsApp normally. The AI works in parallel.
whatsapp.webhookActiveDesc: The AI receives and replies to messages coming to this WhatsApp automatically.
whatsapp.webhookMissingDesc: Without the webhook the AI never sees inbound messages. Click configure.
whatsapp.webhookReconfigureError: Could not configure the webhook on Evolution
whatsapp.webhookRecreated: Instance was recreated. Scan the QR Code below to pair again.
whatsapp.funnelOnlyOnDesc: The AI only replies to people already registered as leads (came from a webhook, import or added manually). Messages from unknown numbers are saved to the inbox but the AI stays silent.
whatsapp.funnelOnlyOffDesc: The AI replies to any number that messages this WhatsApp, even when there is no lead record.
whatsapp.restartConfirm: Restart the WhatsApp session on Evolution? Keeps the pairing, only reopens the connection. Use this when sends are failing with 'Connection Closed'.
whatsapp.restartOk: Session restarted. Try sending a message again.
whatsapp.restartError: Could not restart. If it persists, click Reconnect and scan the QR again.
whatsapp.restartHint: If messages are failing even though the number is connected, click Restart. It reopens the WhatsApp connection without re-scanning the QR.
whatsapp.diagnoseDesc: Runs fetchInstances, connectionState and whatsappNumbers in sequence and shows the raw response from each. Use when sends fail to see exactly what Evolution is returning.
whatsapp.diagnoseReport: Report (click copy and paste into support chat)

email.title: Email
email.subtitle: Email sending via Resend
email.resendConfigDesc: The AI will use Resend to send emails to leads
email.helpText: Create a Resend account and verify your domain. The AI will use this channel for automatic follow-up via email.
email.fillRequired: Please fill in all required fields
email.inboundDesc: Every new email becomes a lead, the AI replies on its own at any time of day.
email.inboundInstructions: Configure your inbound mail provider (Resend Inbound, Postmark, Mailgun, Zapier or Make) to forward every incoming message to this URL. The AI will reply automatically.
email.inboundSecretHint: When set, we require the x-webhook-secret header to match this value.
email.rotateSecretConfirm: Generate a new secret? The current one stops working immediately.
email.modePlatformSubtitle: Ready to use, no Resend account or DNS setup needed.
email.modeCustomSubtitle: Bring your own Resend account and verified domain.
email.platformDesc: You send directly from {domain} using our Resend account. Pick a sender address below — it is what the lead will see in the email.
email.customDesc: Paste your Resend API key (free up to 3,000 emails/month) and the domain you already verified there. You need to have added the DNS records at your provider.
email.aliasHint: Only lowercase letters, digits and hyphens. Start with a letter. Min 3, max 30 characters.
email.aliasTaken: That address is already in use by another account. Pick a different one.
email.aliasReserved: That address is reserved by the platform. Pick a different one.
email.aliasInvalid: Invalid format. Use lowercase letters, digits and hyphens, starting with a letter.
email.usePlatformInstead: To use our domain, switch to the 'Use platform domain' tab.

sms.title: SMS
sms.subtitle: SMS sending via Twilio
sms.twilioConfigDesc: The AI will use Twilio to send and receive SMS from leads
sms.helpText: Create a Twilio account and buy a number. The AI will use this channel for automatic SMS follow-up.
sms.fillRequired: Please fill in all required fields
sms.inboundDesc: Every inbound message becomes a lead, the AI replies on its own at any time of day.
sms.inboundInstructions: Paste this URL into Twilio Console, Phone Numbers, your number, Messaging, A message comes in. Select HTTP POST.

aiConfig.title: AI Assistant
aiConfig.subtitle: Choose an assistant and provide your company information
aiConfig.chooseAssistantDesc: Each one comes trained with advanced sales techniques. Select what fits your business.
aiConfig.aboutCompanyDesc: Fill in the fields or click "Fill by voice" to dictate everything at once.
aiConfig.aboutCompanyRecording: Say the company name, what it does, products, differentials and target audience. The AI fills everything automatically.
aiConfig.differentialsPlaceholder: What sets you apart? Guarantees, results...
aiConfig.fillCompanyName: Please fill in at least the company name

closer.name: The Closer
closer.desc: SPIN Selling, mental triggers and closing techniques. Converts leads into customers naturally.

consultivo.name: The Consultant
consultivo.desc: Deep consultative approach. Understands the lead's scenario before suggesting anything.

empatico.name: The Nurturer
empatico.desc: Creates deep emotional connection. Makes the lead feel heard before anything else.

agendador.name: The Scheduler
agendador.desc: Qualifies fast and schedules meetings with the human closer. Doesn't sell, ensures the right lead reaches the team.

rapido.name: The Quick One
rapido.desc: Fast and objective responses. No fluff. The lead knows what they want, give the info and the link.
rapido.bestFor: E-commerce, digital products, low ticket, promotions

assistantsTab.title: Custom assistants
assistantsTab.subtitle: Build multiple personas (sales, support, post-sale) and switch the active one whenever you need.
assistantsTab.confirmDelete: Are you sure you want to remove this assistant?
assistantsTab.emptyDescription: Create assistants with their own name, model and prompt. Activate the one currently handling leads.
assistantsTab.promptHint: How it should behave, speak and close. Paste a full prompt here.
assistantsTab.promptPlaceholder: You are Carla, sales consultant at a digital marketing agency...
assistantsTab.temperatureHint: 0 = predictable and dry. 1+ = more creative, more variation.

knowledgeTab.title: Knowledge base
knowledgeTab.subtitle: Files the AI consults to answer leads accurately.
knowledgeTab.tooLarge: File of {size} MB exceeds the 50 MB limit.
knowledgeTab.confirmDelete: Remove this file from the base?
knowledgeTab.emptyDescription: Upload FAQs, price lists, manuals. Plain text (.txt/.md/.csv) is read directly by the AI.
knowledgeTab.fileHint: Up to 50 MB. PDF, DOCX, TXT, CSV, MD, XLSX, PPTX.
knowledgeTab.descriptionHint: What the AI will learn from this file
knowledgeTab.descriptionPlaceholder: Full price table, volume discounts and refund policy
knowledgeTab.previewEmpty: This file has no extractable text. Scanned PDFs need a text layer, or upload as an image (PNG/JPG) so the system can OCR it.
knowledgeTab.previewHint: This is exactly the content the AI reads from this file during conversations. If something important is missing, edit the original and re-upload.

mediaTab.title: Media the AI can send
mediaTab.subtitle: Each file has a name, description and send instruction. The AI picks the right moment.
mediaTab.tooLarge: File of {size} MB exceeds the 100 MB limit.
mediaTab.emptyDescription: Upload catalogs, demo videos, audio testimonials, product images. The AI sends them when it makes sense.
mediaTab.fileHint: Up to 100 MB. Image, video, audio or document.
mediaTab.nameHint: Short name the AI will reference internally
mediaTab.descriptionHint: Describe the contents so the AI knows what it's sending
mediaTab.descriptionPlaceholder: 4-page PDF with all plans, prices, terms and this month's bonuses.
mediaTab.sendInstructionLabel: When should the AI send it?
mediaTab.sendInstructionHint: Plain language, the AI follows your call
mediaTab.sendInstructionPlaceholder: When the lead asks about pricing, or after they confirm interest and ask for more offer details.

settings.title: Account
settings.subtitle: Manage your profile, workspace, team and security in one place.

profile.title: Personal profile
profile.subtitle: How you appear to teammates and clients across the platform.
profile.emailHint: Contact support to change your email.

workspace.title: Workspace
workspace.subtitle: Your company's identity, timezone and default language.
workspace.name: Workspace name
workspace.slugHint: Your unique workspace slug, cannot be changed.
workspace.planSubtitle: User cap, features and subscription term.

team.title: Team
team.subtitle: Invite people to collaborate inside this workspace.
team.atCap: Limit reached, upgrade your plan to add more.
team.inviteSubtitle: We generate the password and a ready-to-send message.
team.existingUserAdded: Existing user added to the workspace.
team.credsHint: Recommend changing the password on first sign-in.

security.passwordSubtitle: Update your sign-in password. We recommend at least 12 characters.
security.passwordMinLength: Password must be at least 8 characters.
security.signOutDesc: You'll be signed out and sent back to the login screen.

errors.weak_password: Password must be at least 8 characters.
errors.member_limit_reached: Member limit reached for this plan.
errors.already_member: This email is already on the team.
errors.cannot_remove_owner: You can't remove the workspace owner.

hub.title: Help center
hub.subtitle: Everything you need to put your AI to work on autopilot.
hub.startHereDesc: First time on the platform? Follow the full setup guide. In 20-30 minutes your AI is trained, connected and replying to leads.
hub.setupDesc: Training, knowledge base, channels, funnel. Everything you must configure before the first lead arrives.
hub.trainingDesc: Set tone and persona, define triggers, upload the knowledge base the AI will reference.
hub.channelsDesc: WhatsApp via Evolution API, Email via Resend, SMS via Twilio. Step by step for each.
hub.funnelDesc: First message, follow-up cadence, transfer rules and calendar integration.
hub.webhooksDesc: Meta Lead Ads, Typeform, Zapier, Make, n8n or direct API. How to push leads into the platform.

setup.title: Full setup guide
setup.subtitle: End-to-end walkthrough to make your AI ready to sell.
setup.step1ShortTitle: Train the AI and upload the knowledge base
setup.step4ShortTitle: What to do when something goes wrong
setup.step1Title: Train the AI and upload the knowledge base
setup.step1Intro: Before any lead arrives, the AI needs to know WHO it is, what your company sells and the rules of the game. Without that it sends generic messages that do not convert.
setup.s1Sub1P1: In AI Assistant you set the agent's name, its role and its tone of voice. These three fields drive every message it sends and directly shape how the lead perceives you.
s1Sub1Bullets: Name: use a real human name (e.g. Carlos, Maria, Luna), never 'Virtual Assistant' or 'AI Bot'. The lead feels they are talking to a person.
Role: 'Sales consultant', 'Product specialist', 'Customer success'. Match the style of your business.
Tone: professional, friendly, casual or formal. Premium B2B should skip emojis. Younger B2C may allow them.
Language: lock to EN, PT, ES or IT if you sell in a single market. Auto mode replies in the lead's language, useful for multilingual markets.
setup.s1Sub1Tip: Selling across multiple countries? Create one Assistant per market with the language locked instead of leaving it on Auto. Copy reads more natural and objections drop.
setup.s1Sub2P1: In the 'AI Training' field, write in plain language the context the AI must master: product description, differentiators, target audience, common objections, pricing band, guarantee policy, etc. The more specific, the better.
s1Sub2Steps: Open AI Assistant from the sidebar.
In 'AI Training', describe your company in 4 to 8 paragraphs. Focus on: what you sell, to whom, the outcome the customer gets, and what makes you different.
Include the top three selling arguments (proof, authority, urgency).
List common objections and the right response to each. E.g. 'If the lead says it is expensive, show the ROI within X days.'
Define what the AI must NEVER do (e.g. never invent pricing, never promise deadlines, never speak about competitors).
setup.s1Sub2Warn: Do not write 'sell aggressively' or 'be pushy'. Language models read that the wrong way. Instead describe the concrete BEHAVIOR you want (e.g. 'always end with a qualifying question').
setup.s1Sub3P1: Anything you upload to the knowledge base is indexed and made available to the AI when it composes a reply. Accepts PDF, DOCX, XLSX, PPTX, TXT and images (PNG/JPG/WebP, with automatic OCR via GPT-4o-mini).
s1Sub3Bullets: Product catalog with SKU, price and technical description.
Commission table or B2B special terms.
Full FAQ of objections and answers.
Real screenshots of sales conversations that closed deals (trains tone).
Customer success stories and testimonials (AI quotes when relevant).
setup.s1Sub3P2: Per-file size cap: 100 MB. File count: unlimited. Scanned PDFs without a text layer are NOT readable, convert to text first.
s1Sub3Steps: In AI Assistant, scroll to 'Knowledge base'.
Click 'Add file' and pick the PDF, spreadsheet or image.
Give it a clear title and pick a category (catalog, FAQ, policy, etc.).
Wait for the green 'Indexed' badge. An amber warning means the format is supported but extraction failed (scanned PDF, corrupted file).
To check what was extracted, click the eye icon. If it looks empty or garbled, re-upload in a better format.
setup.s1Sub3Tip: Build a single file called 'SALES_BIBLE.md' containing everything: product, differentiators, FAQ, scripts. The AI uses it as a central reference and stays more consistent.
s1Checklist: AI name, role and tone set
Language locked (if you sell in one country only)
List of common objections with correct responses
Knowledge base with at least one catalog or FAQ file
setup.step2Title: Connect the channels the AI will use
setup.step2Intro: Without a connected channel the AI has nowhere to send messages. WhatsApp is the most critical (90% of ad leads). Email and SMS are great as fallbacks.
setup.s2Sub1P1: We use Evolution API under the hood, which connects to WhatsApp via Baileys (same tech as WhatsApp Web). You scan a QR Code with your phone and the AI starts sending and receiving messages.
s2Sub1Steps: Go to Channels > WhatsApp in the sidebar.
Click 'Connect WhatsApp'. Wait for the QR Code to appear (about 5 seconds).
On your phone open WhatsApp > Settings > Linked devices > Link a device.
Scan the QR Code. The badge turns green showing 'Connected +number'.
Confirm 'Webhook active' is also shown (it is what tells Evolution to notify us when the lead replies).
setup.s2Sub1Tip: Use a dedicated WhatsApp number for the AI, not your personal one. Personal numbers risk being banned because of multiple concurrent sessions, and you don't want to lose your main number.
s2Sub1WhenFails: 'No WhatsApp' (red): the lead's number is not on WhatsApp. Resending will not help, reach them via email or SMS.
'Connection Closed' / 'Instance offline' (amber): the connection dropped. Click Restart on the WhatsApp card. If that doesn't fix it, Reconnect and scan the QR again.
If the error persists after reconnecting, the linked phone may have removed the device (Phone > Linked devices, check if the device is still listed).
setup.s2Sub2P1: For email the AI uses Resend, free up to 3,000 emails/month. You must own a verified domain (free gmail/outlook addresses won't work).
s2Sub2Steps: Create a free account at resend.com and verify your domain (add 3 DNS records, takes ~10min).
In Channels > Email, paste the Resend API key (format 're_...').
Set the sender name (e.g. 'Carlos from Sweet Professional') and the sender email (e.g. 'carlos@yourbrand.com').
Use 'Send test email' to confirm it works before the first lead arrives.
setup.s2Sub2Tip: Using a custom domain (yourbrand.com) drastically improves deliverability. Email from @gmail.com hits spam, email from @yourbrand.com lands in the inbox.
setup.s2Sub3P1: SMS is great for re-engaging a lead who went silent on WhatsApp. Works with Twilio (pay per SMS sent, ~$0.04 each).
s2Sub3Steps: Create an account at twilio.com and buy a number (~$1/month).
Copy the Account SID, Auth Token and Phone Number from your account.
In Channels > SMS, paste the three fields.
Save and trigger a test SMS.
s2Checklist: WhatsApp connected (green badge and Webhook active)
Email with custom domain configured (optional but recommended)
SMS configured if used as fallback (optional)
At least ONE active channel before opening the funnel
setup.step3Intro: The funnel defines HOW the AI approaches the lead: which message comes first, on which channels, how many follow-ups, when to hand off to a human, and how to book a meeting.
setup.s3Sub1P1: Before anything, pick what the AI must deliver:
s3Sub1Bullets: Close the sale: the AI guides the lead to the checkout/payment link.
Schedule a meeting: the AI qualifies and books a slot on Google Calendar.
Qualify and transfer: the AI asks the initial questions and hands off to a human closer.
Collect data and send material: the AI captures the brief and tells the lead you will send a proposal.
setup.s3Sub2P1: The AI's first message decides whether the lead replies or ignores. You have two modes:
s3Sub2Steps: Instruction mode (recommended): describe the structure of the message in plain language and the AI generates a unique version per lead, using name, context and the knowledge base. E.g. 'Greet by name, mention product X, highlight differentiator Y, end with a qualifying question'.
Exact mode: write the LITERAL message that goes out to everyone. Use {name}, {company} and {campaign} as placeholders.
Either way, prefer messages with 3 to 5 structural parts (validation, hook, value, social proof, question). The AI automatically splits them into separate WhatsApp bubbles.
setup.s3Sub2Tip: A larger, well-structured message converts more than an ultra-short one. The lead sees the value upfront. Use detailed Instruction mode and the AI honors the structure.
setup.s3Sub3P1: Lead didn't reply? The AI fires follow-ups in the cadence you set. You can have up to 10 follow-ups, each on its own channel and tone.
s3Sub3Steps: In Pipeline, go to the 'Follow-ups' section.
Add the first follow-up: e.g. 'WhatsApp, 24h later, instruction: remind the topic and ask a new question'.
Add the second: e.g. 'Email, 3 days later, instruction: share a relevant case study and propose a demo'.
Add the third: e.g. 'SMS, 7 days later, instruction: last friendly attempt'.
The higher the attempt, the lighter the approach. No pressure.
setup.s3Sub3Warn: More than 5 follow-ups without a response = spam complaint risk. Tune the cadence for your market.
setup.s3Sub4P1: For the AI to receive leads automatically, plug your source (Meta Ads, Typeform, Zapier, etc.) into the platform's universal webhook.
s3Sub4Steps: In Pipeline, click 'See step-by-step connection guide' or go to Help > Webhooks.
Paste it into the source tool (Meta Lead Ads, Typeform, Zapier).
Configure the field mapping: name, email, phone, source, metadata.
Fire a test lead and confirm it appears in Leads.
s3Checklist: Funnel goal chosen (close, book a meeting, etc.)
First message defined (Instruction mode with 3+ structural parts)
Webhook connected to the lead source (Meta Ads / Typeform / etc.)
Test with 1 real lead before scaling traffic
setup.step4Intro: The most common issues and how to fix them in 2 minutes.
setup.t1Q: The AI sent only the greeting and cut the rest of the message.
setup.t1ATitle: Usually the operator instruction is ambiguous.
t1Steps: Rewrite the instruction as a numbered list: '1. Greet. 2. Mention X. 3. Highlight Y. 4. Ask Z.'
Be explicit about length: 'Use 4 short paragraphs, one per item above.'
Save, go to Leads and click 'Resend first contact' on a failed lead to see the new result.
setup.t2Q: The AI replied in another language even though I configured English.
setup.t2ATitle: You are using an Assistant overlay and the overlay language is overriding.
t2Steps: Confirm the language is locked in the 'Assistant language' field.
If you have a named assistant active, make sure the language is set on the base Assistant (the overlay should not touch it, but check).
Already-generated messages won't change. Only new ones go out in the right language.
setup.t3Q: Getting 'Connection Closed' or messages are failing.
setup.t3ATitle: The WhatsApp link dropped even though the UI says Connected.
t3Steps: Click the Restart button (amber). It forces Evolution to reopen the connection without losing the pairing.
Wait 5 seconds and try to resend a message from Leads > Resend first contact.
If still failing, click Reconnect and scan the QR again. Check on the phone that WhatsApp Web wasn't unlinked under Linked devices.
setup.t4Q: The AI is replying to people who aren't even my leads.
setup.t4ATitle: The 'Only reply to funnel leads' gate is off.
t4Steps: Turn on the 'Only reply to funnel leads' toggle (should turn green).
Now the AI only talks to leads that came via webhook, import or manual entry. Messages from unknown numbers stay in the inbox without an auto-reply.
setup.t5Q: A lead shows 'No WhatsApp' in red.
setup.t5ATitle: The lead's number is not on WhatsApp.
t5Steps: Resending won't help, Evolution already verified.
If you have the lead's email, open the conversation, pause the AI and email them manually or enable Email in the Pipeline.
If it's a fake campaign lead, mark as Lost to clean the funnel.
Consider adding number validation before submit in your forms (Typeform and Meta Lead Ads accept regex).
setup.supportDesc: If none of the above fixed it, send a screenshot of the error and the affected lead ID to support. The more context, the faster the answer.

webhooks.title: How to connect your lead sources
webhooks.subtitle: Paste this URL into the tool that captures your leads. Every lead sent becomes a contact in the system and the AI starts handling it automatically.
webhooks.universalUrlDesc: Works with any tool that fires webhooks (Meta Lead Ads, Google Ads, Typeform, Zapier, Make, Unbounce, and more). Send the JSON below, we ingest it.
webhooks.metaStep1: In Meta Business Manager open Tools, Lead Ads Forms.
webhooks.metaStep2: Create or edit your form and copy its ID.
webhooks.metaStep3: Connect your Page and enable the webhook pointing at the universal URL.
webhooks.metaStep4: Test by sending a sample lead through Graph API Explorer.
webhooks.typeformStep1: In your form click Connect, Webhooks.
webhooks.typeformStep2: Paste the universal URL and enable the webhook.
webhooks.typeformStep3: Fields name, email and phone are detected automatically.
webhooks.zapierStep1: Create a Zap (or scenario) with the lead-capture trigger.
webhooks.zapierStep2: Add a Webhooks by Zapier, POST action.
webhooks.zapierStep3: Paste the universal URL as destination.
webhooks.zapierStep4: Map the lead fields to the JSON shape above.
webhooks.genericStep1: POST to the universal URL with Content-Type application/json.
webhooks.genericStep2: Send the body in the JSON shape above.
webhooks.genericStep3: Each call creates a lead and triggers the AI's first message automatically.
webhooks.testDesc: Run this command in your terminal to simulate a lead arriving. In seconds you'll see it show up on the Leads page.
webhooks.testHint: If you get 201 Created, it's working. If 404, check the accountId in the URL. If 401, check your sending channel is active.
